turningPoints.play: McCollum 7' Driving Floating Jump Shot (30 PTS)

Game Drama Score.details: Decided by 1 point with 3 lead changes and 37 ties. The game saw 13 major win probability swings, and the losing team's chances peaked at 98%.

Most Valuable Play.details: Q4, 0:41.9, Johnson (ATL): Johnson REBOUND (Off:1 Def:7). It swung the win probability by +44.9 points.

Most Damaging Play.details: Q4, 0:05.6, McCollum (ATL): MISS McCollum Free Throw 1 of 2. It swung the win probability by -43.2 points.

Clutch-Time Scoring Summary.value: Scoring over the final five minutes: home team 12, away team 23.

recap.Biggest Turning Point: The game turned at Q4, 1:21: Brunson 26' 3PT Pullup Jump Shot (26 PTS). That single play moved NYK's chance of winning from 25% to 54%. No other moment shifted the game more, and it went NYK's way.
recap.Player Impact: No one influenced the outcome more than McCollum of ATL. Across 43 plays, his actions moved the win probability by a combined 133 percentage points, the most of any player on the floor.
recap.Comeback Reality: The tensest moment for a comeback came at Q4, 6:05, with ATL down 5 points. The model put their chance of pulling it off at 11%. In other words: difficult, but not nothing.
recap.Hidden Momentum: The strongest run that didn't fully show on the scoreboard came at Q3, 11:14, with the game tilting NYK's way. The play in the middle of it: Towns 27' 3PT Jump Shot (7 PTS) (Hart 5 AST).
recap.Model Note: These numbers come from ClutchCast's champion model, the winner of a competition between six models. The winner is judged on how honest its probabilities are on games it never trained on, not just how often it picks the right winner.
